name: Budget Variance Explainer
description: Turn a budget-vs-actual data dump into a decision-ready variance explanation — every deviation quantified, causally explained, classified as timing vs. run-rate, and routed to an owner for corrective action.
author: accilium

variables.name: BUDGET_ACTUAL_DATA
variables.description: The budget vs. actual data (paste as text, CSV, or markdown table) — one line per budget item.
variables.example: Personnel: budget 1.2m, actual 1.35m; Travel: budget 80k, actual 52k; Marketing: budget 300k, actual 410k...

variables.name: PERIOD
variables.example: Q2 2026 YTD vs. approved FY2026 budget

variables.name: CONTEXT
variables.description: Known events that may explain deviations (hiring freezes, price changes, delayed projects, one-offs).
variables.example: Two open roles unfilled since March; marketing ran an unplanned campaign in May for the product launch

variables.name: THRESHOLD
variables.description: Materiality threshold for what counts as a variance worth individual commentary.
variables.example: Anything beyond ±5% or ±25k, whichever is lower

example_input: BUDGET_ACTUAL_DATA: marketing 300k budget vs 410k actual; PERIOD: Q2 2026 vs budget; CONTEXT: unplanned launch campaign; THRESHOLD: ±5%
